drugs.notes: Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Dose rounded to nearest 1000 IU. Maximum cumulative dose 500,000 IU in patients under 60 years. Hydrocortisone 100mg IV when required for bleomycin-related reactions.
Intravenous infusion in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes. Dose banded (multi syringe 20mg/ml). Escalated dose Level 1; may be stepped down through levels 1250, 1100, 950, 800, 650 mg/m².
Intravenous infusion in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes. Dose banded (10mg/ml). Dacarbazine substituted for procarbazine in this regimen.

Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Dose banded (multi syringe 2mg/ml). Maximum lifetime cumulative dose 450mg/m²; 400mg/m² if prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy. Discontinue if cardiac failure develops.
Intravenous infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes. Dose banded (20mg/ml). Escalated dose Level 1; may be stepped down through levels 200, 175, 150, 125, 100 mg/m².
Oral, days 1-14. Available as 5mg and 25mg tablets, rounded to nearest 5mg. Take in morning with or after food. Day 1 dose administered in clinic as pre-medication; days 2-14 self-administered.

Intravenous bolus in 50ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 10 minutes. Dose banded (1mg/ml). Maximum dose 2mg. Reduce to 1mg/m² for NCI-CTC grade 2 motor or grade 3 sensory neurotoxicity; stop for higher grades or if toxicity increases despite reduction.

Growth factors mandatory. Filgrastim or bioequivalent 30 million units once daily for 5 days from day 9, or lenograstim or bioequivalent 33.6 million units once daily for 5 days from day 9, subcutaneous.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: WBC <1.0x10⁹/L for >4 days, platelets <25x10⁹/L, or infection/mucositis requiring 2-week delay
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce by one level on five-level scale: 1250→1100→950→800→650 mg/m². If toxic effects in two successive cycles use standard doses (650mg/m²) for all subsequent cycles.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: WBC <1.0x10⁹/L for >4 days, platelets <25x10⁹/L, or infection/mucositis requiring 2-week delay
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce by one level on five-level scale: 200→175→150→125→100 mg/m². If toxic effects in two successive cycles use standard doses (100mg/m²) for all subsequent cycles.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic: Bilirubin 30-51 µmol/L or AST/ALT 60-180 units/L
Hepatic: Bilirubin >51 µmol/L or AST/ALT >180 units/L
Hepatic: Bilirubin 30-51 µmol/L or AST/ALT 60-180 units/L
Hepatic: Bilirubin >51 µmol/L and AST/ALT normal
Hepatic: Bilirubin >51 µmol/L and AST/ALT >180 units/L

dose_modifications.notes: Consider dose reduction in severe renal failure

pre_medications: Ondansetron 8mg oral or intravenous (15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy days 1-3)
Prednisolone 40mg/m² oral (at least 15 minutes prior to chemotherapy days 1-3, also part of chemotherapy schedule)
G-CSF days 9-13 (filgrastim 30 million units SC or lenograstim 33.6 million units SC once daily)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily oral as required (take home)
Allopurinol 300mg once daily oral for 7 days (first cycle only)
Aciclovir 400mg twice daily oral
Co-trimoxazole 960mg once daily oral Monday, Wednesday and Friday
Fluconazole 100mg once daily oral
Mouthwashes per local/national mucositis policy
Proton pump inhibitor or H2 antagonist for high-risk patients
special_warnings: Hodgkin's Lymphoma patients carry lifelong risk of TA-GVHD: irradiated blood products must be used for life; transfusion departments must be notified and patient issued with alert card.
Bleomycin cumulative dose limit: 500,000 international units in patients under 60 years of age. Stop bleomycin if pulmonary symptoms develop until investigated.
Doxorubicin maximum lifetime cumulative dose: 450mg/m² (400mg/m² if prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy). Discontinue if cardiac failure develops.
Vincristine maximum single dose: 2mg.
Growth factors (G-CSF) are mandatory as part of this regimen.
Dacarbazine is a vesicant — extravasation risk.
Dacarbazine substituted for procarbazine in BEACOPDac variant.
Cycles repeat on day 22 provided WBC >2.5x10⁹/L and platelets >80x10⁹/L; day 8 drugs given on schedule regardless of counts.
Cyclophosphamide and etoposide dose reduction follows a five-level scale from escalated to standard doses based on toxicity.
Procarbazine not included in this regimen (dacarbazine replaces it).
NPSA/2008/RRR04 must be followed for intravenous vinca alkaloids.

drugs.notes: Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Dose rounded to nearest 1000 IU. Maximum cumulative dose 500,000 IU in patients under 60 years. Hydrocortisone 100mg IV when required for bleomycin-related reactions.
Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Dose banded (multi syringe 20mg/ml).

Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Dose banded (multi syringe 2mg/ml). Maximum lifetime cumulative dose 450mg/m²; 400mg/m² if prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy. Discontinue if cardiac failure develops.
Intravenous infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes. Dose banded (20mg/ml).
Oral days 1-7. Available as 5mg and 25mg tablets, rounded to nearest 5mg. Take in morning with or after food. Day 1 dose administered in clinic as pre-medication; days 2-7 self-administered.

Oral days 1-7. Available as 50mg capsules. Dose rounded to nearest 25mg for alternate day dosing in ARIA. Has weak MAOI activity — avoid alcohol and foods rich in tyramine; do not use with other MAOIs.

Intravenous bolus in 50ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 10 minutes. Dose banded (1mg/ml). Maximum dose 2mg. Reduce to 1mg/m² for NCI-CTC grade 2 motor or grade 3 sensory neurotoxicity; stop for higher grades or if toxicity increases.
Growth factors mandatory. Filgrastim or bioequivalent 30 million units once daily for 5 days from day 9, or lenograstim 33.6 million units once daily for 5 days from day 9, subcutaneous.

Aciclovir 400mg twice daily oral

Mouthwashes per local/national mucositis policy
Proton pump inhibitor or H2 antagonist for high-risk patients
special_warnings: Hodgkin's Lymphoma patients carry lifelong risk of TA-GVHD: irradiated blood products must be used for life; transfusion departments must be notified and patient issued with alert card.
Bleomycin cumulative dose limit: 500,000 international units in patients under 60 years of age. Stop bleomycin if pulmonary symptoms develop until investigated.
Doxorubicin maximum lifetime cumulative dose: 450mg/m² (400mg/m² if prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy). Discontinue if cardiac failure develops.

Growth factors (G-CSF) are mandatory as part of this regimen.

drugs.notes: Cycle 1: days 1 and 2. Cycles 2-6: days 1 and 2. Intravenous infusion in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes. Dose banded (2.5mg/ml). Vesicant.

Cycle 1: days 1, 8, and 15 (1000mg each). Cycles 2-6: day 1 only (1000mg). Intravenous infusion in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9%. Rate per protocol — start at 50mg/hr, escalate in 50mg/hr increments every 30 minutes to maximum 400mg/hr. Followed by obinutuzumab maintenance (separate protocol, 56-day cycles).

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: Neutrophils <0.5x10⁹/L or febrile neutropenia — 1st occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until recovery and give 75% of original dose

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: Neutrophils <0.5x10⁹/L or febrile neutropenia — 2nd occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until recovery and give 50% of original dose
Delay until recovery and give 75% of original dose
Delay until recovery and give 50% of original dose
No information available; use with caution
Delay until resolved to grade 2 or below, then reduce to 75%; if recurs further reduce to 50% or discontinue

dose_modifications.condition: Obinutuzumab: Grade 2 non-haematological toxicity delaying treatment by >4 weeks
Obinutuzumab: Grade 4 toxicity, severe haemorrhage, severe skin reaction, pneumonitis, severe arrhythmia, viral hepatitis or serious infection

pre_medications: Ondansetron 8mg oral or intravenous (15-30 minutes prior to bendamustine day 1)
Methylprednisolone sodium succinate 80mg intravenous (60 minutes prior to obinutuzumab — cycle 1 day 1 and for patients with grade 3 infusion reactions or lymphocytes >25x10⁹/L)
Chlorphenamine 10mg intravenous (60 minutes prior to obinutuzumab — cycle 1 day 1 and all subsequent doses)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral (60 minutes prior to obinutuzumab — all doses)
Sodium chloride 0.9% 500ml IV infusion over 60 minutes (prior to obinutuzumab)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily oral as required (take home day 1)
Ondansetron 8mg twice daily oral for 3 days (take home)
Co-trimoxazole 960mg once daily oral Monday, Wednesday and Friday whilst on bendamustine
Allopurinol 300mg once daily for 7 days (if high TLS risk; kept as short as possible due to SJS risk with bendamustine)
special_warnings: Patients treated with bendamustine carry a lifelong risk of TA-GVHD: irradiated blood products must be used for life; transfusion departments must be notified and patient issued with alert card.
Obinutuzumab in combination with bendamustine followed by obinutuzumab maintenance is for follicular lymphoma not responding to or progressing during/within 6 months of rituximab-containing regimen.
Progressive Multifocal Leukoencephalopathy (PML) has been reported with obinutuzumab — monitor for neurological/cognitive symptoms.
Tumour Lysis Syndrome (TLS) risk with obinutuzumab — prophylaxis with adequate hydration and allopurinol/rasburicase starting 12-24 hours prior to infusion for high-risk patients.
Obinutuzumab infusion related reactions are particularly severe in patients with high tumour burden or high circulating lymphocyte count (>25x10⁹/L).
Stevens-Johnson syndrome and toxic epidermal necrolysis reported with concomitant bendamustine and allopurinol — monitor skin reactions closely.
Bendamustine metabolism involves CYP1A2 — potential interactions with fluvoxamine, ciprofloxacin, aciclovir, and cimetidine.
Withhold antihypertensive treatments 12 hours prior to and throughout each obinutuzumab infusion and for 1 hour after due to hypotension risk.
No dose reduction for obinutuzumab based on haematological parameters.
Hepatitis B status must be checked prior to starting treatment.
Rituximab dose reductions NOT permitted.

drugs.notes: Cycle 1: day 1. Cycles 2-6: day 1. Intravenous infusion in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% as per rituximab administration guidelines. Dose rounded to nearest 100mg. Rituximab dose reductions NOT permitted.

Cycle 1: day 2. Cycles 2-6: day 1. Intravenous infusion in 100ml glucose 5% over 90 minutes (first infusion); subsequent infusions may be given over 30 minutes if tolerated. Maximum dose 240mg. Must be administered using a sterile non-pyrogenic low protein binding 0.2µ or 0.22µ filter. Monitor for 90 minutes after first infusion.
Cycle 1: days 2 and 3. Cycles 2-6: days 1 and 2. Intravenous infusion in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes. Dose banded (2.5mg/ml). Vesicant.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: Neutrophils 0.5-1x10⁹/L and recover to ≥1 by day 7
dose_modifications.notes: Resume with no dose reduction if neutrophils recover to 1 on or before day 7

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: Neutrophils 0.5-1x10⁹/L and do not recover to ≥1 by day 7
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce from 90mg/m² to 70mg/m², or from 70mg/m² to 50mg/m². If already reduced to 50mg/m², discontinue bendamustine.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: Platelets 25-75x10⁹/L and recover to ≥75 by day 7
dose_modifications.notes: Resume with no dose reduction if platelets recover to 75 on or before day 7

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: Platelets 25-75x10⁹/L and do not recover to ≥75 by day 7
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce from 90mg/m² to 70mg/m², or from 70mg/m² to 50mg/m². If already reduced to 50mg/m², discontinue bendamustine.
Withhold until resolves to grade 1 or below. If resolves to grade 1 on or before day 14, restart at permanently reduced dose of 1.4mg/kg. If recurs or does not resolve within 14 days, discontinue.

pre_medications: Chlorphenamine 10mg intravenous (30 minutes prior to polatuzumab and rituximab)
Hydrocortisone 100mg intravenous (30 minutes prior to rituximab only)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral (30 minutes prior to polatuzumab and rituximab)
Ondansetron 8mg oral or intravenous (15-30 minutes prior to treatment — day 2 cycle 1 and day 1 each cycle thereafter)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily oral as required (take home)
Ondansetron 8mg twice daily for 3 days oral (take home)
Co-trimoxazole 960mg once daily oral Monday, Wednesday and Friday
Allopurinol 300mg once daily for 14 days (if high TLS risk; keep course as short as possible due to SJS risk)
special_warnings: Patients treated with bendamustine carry a lifelong risk of TA-GVHD: irradiated blood products must be used for life; transfusion departments must be notified and patient issued with alert card.
Polatuzumab vedotin is CD79b-directed antibody-drug conjugate — CD79b status relevant to this therapy.
Progressive Multifocal Leukoencephalopathy (PML) reported with polatuzumab and rituximab — monitor for neurological/cognitive symptoms at regular intervals.
Rituximab dose reductions NOT permitted; polatuzumab and rituximab are withheld but not dose reduced for haematological toxicity.
Stevens-Johnson syndrome and toxic epidermal necrolysis reported with concomitant bendamustine and allopurinol — monitor skin reactions closely.
Peripheral neuropathy risk with polatuzumab — monitor and dose reduce/discontinue as per protocol.
Bendamustine is unlicensed for DLBCL indication; MHRA warning (July 2017) regarding increased mortality and need to monitor for opportunistic infections and hepatitis B reactivation.
Bendamustine metabolism involves CYP1A2 — potential interactions with fluvoxamine, ciprofloxacin, aciclovir, and cimetidine.
Check hepatitis B status prior to starting rituximab.
Polatuzumab hepatotoxicity — resembles hepatocellular injury including elevation of transaminases and/or bilirubin.
First infusion of polatuzumab should be given over 90 minutes; monitor for 90 minutes afterwards.

drugs.notes: Intravenous infusion in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% as per rituximab administration guidelines. Dose rounded to nearest 100mg. Rituximab dose reductions NOT permitted.
Intravenous infusion in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes. Dose banded (2.5mg/ml). Vesicant.

special_warnings: Patients treated with bendamustine carry a lifelong risk of TA-GVHD: irradiated blood products must be used for life; transfusion departments must be notified and patient issued with alert card.
Rituximab dose reductions NOT permitted; dose modifications based on haematological parameters apply to bendamustine only.
Progressive Multifocal Leukoencephalopathy (PML) has been reported with rituximab — monitor for neurological/cognitive symptoms.

Bendamustine metabolism involves CYP1A2 — potential interactions with fluvoxamine, ciprofloxacin, aciclovir, and cimetidine.
Check hepatitis B status prior to starting rituximab.
There are multiple versions of this protocol in use — ensure the correct protocol for the relevant diagnosis is used.

drugs.notes: Intravenous infusion in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes. Dose banded (2.5mg/ml). Vesicant. Consider initiating at lower doses in elderly, frail or heavily pre-treated patients. Indication: indolent NHL where disease progressed during or within 6 months of rituximab-based therapy.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay until recovery and give 75% of original dose

pre_medications: Ondansetron 8mg oral or intravenous (15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy day 1)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily oral as required (take home day 1)
Ondansetron 8mg twice daily for 3 days oral (take home from evening of day 1)
Allopurinol 300mg once daily for 14 days (if high TLS risk; keep course as short as possible due to SJS risk)
Mouthwashes per local/national mucositis policy
Proton pump inhibitor or H2 antagonist for high-risk patients
special_warnings: Patients treated with bendamustine carry a lifelong risk of TA-GVHD: irradiated blood products must be used for life; transfusion departments must be notified and patient issued with alert card.

Stevens-Johnson syndrome and toxic epidermal necrolysis reported with concomitant bendamustine and allopurinol — monitor skin reactions closely; withhold bendamustine if mucocutaneous reaction suspected.
Bendamustine metabolism involves CYP1A2 — potential interactions with fluvoxamine, ciprofloxacin, aciclovir, and cimetidine.
There are multiple versions of this protocol in use — ensure the correct protocol for the relevant diagnosis is used.

drugs.notes: 300mg/m²/day orally, days 1-28 (continuous). Available as 75mg soft capsules. Dose banded per BSA table; e.g. BSA 0.88-1.12m² = 300mg/day (4 capsules). Take once daily with a meal; swallow whole. Continue until disease progression or intolerance. Twelve cycles set in ARIA.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay until triglycerides controlled below 4.5 mmol/L
300mg/m²/day may be adjusted down to 200mg/m²/day then to 100mg/m²/day or temporarily suspended. When toxicity controlled, doses may be carefully readjusted upward.

pre_medications: No specific pre-medications mandated
Fenofibrate or other lipid-lowering therapy recommended if hypertriglyceridaemia develops
Thyroid replacement therapy if hypothyroidism develops
special_warnings: Bexarotene is a potent teratogen — women of childbearing potential must use a reliable non-hormonal form of contraception during treatment and for one month after stopping (bexarotene may reduce efficacy of oestroprogestive contraceptives).
Thyroid function monitoring: TSH and total T4 at baseline then monthly — hypothyroidism is common and requires replacement therapy.
Fasting lipid monitoring (triglycerides and cholesterol): baseline, then weekly until stabilised (usually 2-4 weeks), then at least monthly — maintain triglycerides below 3.4 mmol/L.
Bexarotene causes photosensitivity — minimise exposure to sunlight and artificial UV light during treatment.
Bexarotene is related to vitamin A — limit intake to ≤15,000 IU/day.
Doses >650mg/m²/day have not been evaluated in patients with CTCL.
Treatment should be continued as long as the patient is deriving benefit.
Bexarotene should only be prescribed under the supervision of a consultant oncologist or haematologist.
NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed in relation to oral chemotherapy.
Bexarotene interacts with many agents — always check for drug interactions. Can induce metabolic enzymes potentially reducing efficacy of other medications.
FBC and LFTs weekly for first month then monthly; thyroid function monthly.
